---
title: "Explorations"
---

All explorations — finished, cancelled, in-progress, or not started.

## Lake-temperature trajectory heterogeneity

::: {.align-center .text-xl}
[prose](./warming-acceleration/prose/is-there-a-hiatus.qmd) ·
[draft](./warming-acceleration/draft/01-global-kinematics.qmd) ·
[log](./warming-acceleration/log/index.qmd)
:::

<style>
.align-center {
  text-align: center;
}
.text-xl {
  font-size: 1.25rem;
}
</style>

How heterogeneous are reconstructed lake surface water temperature trajectories
across time and space, and what seasonal/ice contexts characterize cooling
trajectories?

92,245 lakes, GLAST dataset, 1981–2020.

Methods: raw annual and moving-window Theil--Sen trends; STL-based low-frequency
PCA; descriptive PCA-score typology; modular cooling/ice diagnostics.

GLAST is a reconstructed/corrected LSWT product, not direct in-situ observation.
The active work distinguishes descriptive trajectory structure from causal
attribution.
